title: strophostyles helvola var. missouriensis
intro: Strophostyles helvola var. missouriensis, also known as Missouri bean or Missouri hopvine, is a perennial vine of the legume family, characterized by its climbing habit and small, white to purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating a variety of health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Strophostyles helvola var. missouriensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
